vitals.date_specific: 2026: 32km/45km Aug 7 (Oxberg-Mora), 92km main race Aug 8 (Sälen-Mora, via Berga by)

terrain.surface: ~97% forest road/gravel, ~3% asphalt on the 92km main race (verified 2026 organizer figures) — this is an MTB/gravel event, not a paved road course

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 61. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 57. WAVE 10 FLAG (2026-07-24): road_surface=1 ('fresh tarmac throughout' per config/dimensions.json rubric) directly conflicts with verified 2026 course data showing ~97% unpaved forest road/gravel on the main race — dimension not changed per standing rule against silent numeric edits, but this is almost certainly a mis-scored dimension pending human review.

course_description.character: The bike edition of Sweden's legendary Vasaloppet ski race, run by Vasaloppsföreningen Sälen-Mora (owned by IFK Mora and Sälens IF) since 2009 — inspired partly by Norway's Birkebeinerrittet. Three distances: 32km and 45km (Oxberg to Mora, Aug 7) and the 92km main race (Sälen to Mora via Berga by, Aug 8). Despite this database's road-catalog placement, the main race is roughly 97% unpaved — forest roads, gravel, classic ski trail, and constructed boardwalks over marshland — with only about 3km of asphalt. This is a gravel/forest-road event wearing a gran fondo's clothes, not a paved sportive.
course_description.signature_challenge: The opening climb: 178m of gain over the first 3km, up to the course's 528m high point — then close to 90km of forest road, gravel and boardwalk before Mora.
suffering_zones: Opening 3km climb (178m gain to 528m high point) right off the start in Sälen.

Constructed boardwalks over marshland sections of the classic Vasaloppet ski trail — a road-oriented rider will find this course technical throughout, and even gravel riders report elevated puncture/crash risk on rough sections.
Congestion and limited passing room around the start and finish, per rider accounts, plus a real risk of mud or rerouted sections if heavy rain hits.

logistics.lodging_strategy: The event runs during Vasaloppet's Summer Week (early August) in the Sälen-Mora corridor.

history.origin_story: Vasaloppet tested a bike route in 2008 and launched Cykelvasan in 2009, partly inspired by Norway's Birkebeinerrittet bike race — it drew nearly 2,000 riders in its debut year.
history.reputation: A well-established Vasaloppet-family event, but its own organizer material and course pages have had inconsistent asphalt/gravel percentage figures year to year — treat any single-source vitals figure with some caution.

biased_opinion.summary: Cykelvasan is a real event with genuine Vasaloppet pedigree — nonprofit-run since 2009, part of Swedish endurance-sport culture, drawing nearly 2,000 riders in its 2009 debut. The problem isn't the race, it's the label: roughly 97% of the 92km main course is forest road, gravel, ski-trail and marshland boardwalk, not pavement. A road cyclist showing up expecting a gran fondo on tarmac will be riding the wrong bike. This is a gravel/MTB event that happens to share a start-finish town pairing with road cycling's vocabulary.
strengths: Established Vasaloppet organizational pedigree — a nonprofit organizer with 15+ years running this specific event
Three distance options (32/45/92km) that let riders self-select difficulty
A forest route through Dalarna tied to the Vasaloppet ski legacy
weaknesses: Roughly 97% unpaved on the main course — this is not a road gran fondo, and the surface mismatch is the single most important thing a road-focused rider needs to know before entering
No reliable bike-specific total elevation figure is currently published by the organizer — only a partial 178m opening-climb figure is confirmed
Congestion around start/finish and mud risk in wet years, per rider accounts
biased_opinion.bottom_line: If you're a gravel or all-road rider looking for a historic Nordic forest event, this is worth a look. If you came here expecting a paved gran fondo, bring a gravel bike or look elsewhere — this database's road classification undersells how off-road this event actually is.

final_verdict.one_liner: A historic Vasaloppet-family event that's roughly 97% gravel/forest road — a gravel race mislabeled as a road gran fondo.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Race it on a gravel bike, not a road bike — the surface mismatch with a 'gran fondo' listing is real. If you want the Vasaloppet-family history and don't mind mixed terrain, it's worth entering.
final_verdict.alternatives: For a genuinely paved Swedish/Nordic road fondo, look elsewhere in the catalog; for more forest/gravel Nordic ultra-endurance events, Norway's Birkebeinerrittet (the event that partly inspired Cykelvasan) is a natural comparison.

catalog_flags.status_note: Taxonomy flag: 2026 organizer-verified course data puts the 92km main race at ~97% unpaved (forest road, gravel, ski trail, marshland boardwalk) — this is an MTB/gravel event, not a paved road gran fondo. terrain.surface corrected to reflect reality; fondo_rating.discipline left as gran_fondo per wave policy (numeric/taxonomy reclassification is a human call, not an automated edit).

videos.title: Vasaloppet på cykel
videos.description:
Phooo what a day ❄️❄️❄️
First sunday in March, we, here in Sweden have this super big ski race Vasaloppet. 90km race from Sälen to Mora.
We dont do ski we do bike so we had fun here on our own turf, 90km Vasaloppet on bike.
The problem wasnt the 90km the problem was the crazy wet cold but we finaly came home in one pice...
...no amputation... so far ✌🏻😎
